other_observers: Brandon Percival and David Chartier; We all saw it at about the same time, probably Brandon saw it first.
description: A towhee type sparrow with its black hood and bib, white belly with chestnut flanks, and dark dorsal surface to its long tail. The back and wings were black with white-centered black tertials, and a white "flag" that shows prominently at the base of the primaries. The eye was red.Behaviors: Sang from top of a short tree but moslty foraged actively on the ground moving from the ranch house at Chico HQ to the dam of little HQ pond to the front yard of the ranch house to the HQ parking lot to the big red barn to the north of HQ almost always on the ground.Call: song was sung from the top of a short Siberian elm. It was noticably higher in pitch than the song of SPTO and it ended in a trill.Plumage: alternate
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
